articles.title: From Near Extinction to Internet Fame: Why Bald Eagle Cams Are Booming
articles.summary: Bald eagle nest cams attract millions of viewers who track every feeding and flight. For superfan Gloria Gajownik, 71, the Decorah eagles and their online community became a surrogate family. The cams also spotlight an incredible comeback: from 417 pairs in 1963 to 71,400 today.

articles.title: Fed Chair Powell's Unprecedented Move: Stay on Board After Term Ends?
articles.summary: As Jerome Powell's term as Fed chair expires May 15, he may remain as a governor — a first since 1948. This sets up a potential "two Popes" clash with Trump nominee Kevin Warsh. The drama unfolds amid a Justice Department probe and a murky economy with 3.3% inflation.

articles.title: Global Military Spending Hits Record $2.88 Trillion — What's the Trade-Off?
articles.summary: In 2025, world military expenditure reached $2.88 trillion, or $350 per person. The U.S. accounted for $954 billion — more than the next six nations combined. Since 1949, America has spent over $53 trillion on defense, over half the global total.
